core_arc: Child finds something that does not belong to them and goes on a short journey to return it to its owner.

personalisation_slots.NAME: Child's first name
personalisation_slots.FRIEND: Friend or sibling name (optional — defaults to a generic name if blank)
personalisation_slots.LOCATION: Setting — park, school, street, garden (mapped from parent's input)
personalisation_slots.INTEREST: Used to flavour the lost item and setting details
personalisation_slots.LOST_ITEM: Derived from interest — e.g., dinosaurs → toy dinosaur, football → shiny football boot
personalisation_slots.OWNER: The person who lost the item — generated name appropriate to setting

scenes.outline: [NAME] is at [LOCATION]. It is a [WEATHER] day. [NAME] is happy.
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] standing in [LOCATION], smiling, looking around. Bright, friendly setting. Establish the character's appearance for consistency across all pages.

scenes.outline: [NAME] sees something on the ground. It is a [LOST_ITEM]. [NAME] picks it up.
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] bending down to look at [LOST_ITEM] on the ground. Curious expression. Same character design as Scene 1.

scenes.outline: [NAME] looks at the [LOST_ITEM]. 'This is not mine,' says [NAME]. 'I must find who it belongs to.'
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] holding [LOST_ITEM], looking thoughtful. Speech bubble or text below showing dialogue.

scenes.outline: [NAME] asks [FRIEND]. 'Is this yours?' 'No,' says [FRIEND]. 'But I will help you look.'
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] showing [LOST_ITEM] to [FRIEND]. Both characters visible. [FRIEND] shaking head but smiling.

scenes.outline: [NAME] and [FRIEND] go to [PLACE_1] to look. They ask [PERSON_1]. 'Not mine,' says [PERSON_1].
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] and [FRIEND] at a new location (e.g., a shop, a bench, a gate). Talking to another character.

scenes.outline: They go to [PLACE_2]. Then [NAME] hears a sound. Someone is looking for something.
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] and [FRIEND] at second location, [NAME] turning head as if hearing something. Hint of another character in the background.

scenes.outline: It is [OWNER]. '[LOST_ITEM]!' says [OWNER]. 'Thank you so much, [NAME]! I have been looking for it all day.'
scenes.illustration_brief: [OWNER] reunited with [LOST_ITEM], looking delighted. [NAME] handing it over proudly. [FRIEND] in background smiling.

scenes.outline: [NAME] and [FRIEND] walk home. [NAME] feels happy. It is good to help.
scenes.illustration_brief: [NAME] and [FRIEND] walking together, smiling, warm light. Peaceful, satisfied feeling. Same setting as Scene 1 in background.

comprehension_questions.inference: How do you think [OWNER] felt when [NAME] gave back the [LOST_ITEM]?
comprehension_questions.vocabulary: What does the word '[VOCAB_WORD]' mean in the story?
comprehension_questions.prediction: What do you think [NAME] would do if they found something again?

writing_worksheet.level_1_2: Trace and write: [TARGET_GRAPHEMES]. Copy the word: [SIMPLE_WORD].
writing_worksheet.level_3_4: Write these words from the story: [WORD_LIST]. Write a sentence about [NAME].
writing_worksheet.level_5_6: Write 2 sentences about what happened in the story. Use the word '[VOCAB_WORD]' in a sentence.

dinosaurs.flavour_detail: The dinosaur had a long tail and big teeth.

space.flavour_detail: The rocket had stars on the side.

unicorns.flavour_detail: The unicorn was pink with a silver horn.

football.flavour_detail: The boot was red with white stripes.

animals.flavour_detail: The rabbit had long floppy ears.

baking.flavour_detail: The book had a cake on the front.

nature.flavour_detail: The jar had holes in the lid for air.

vehicles.flavour_detail: The car had four black wheels.

music.flavour_detail: The drum had a red top and a stick.

art.flavour_detail: The crayons were all the colours of the rainbow.
